engineGuide.h2: Bentley Continental GT Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Bentley Continental GT across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 6.0L Petrol
entries.history: Gen I W12 defined Bentley's modern grand touring era with smooth 12-cylinder power, though thermal cycling challenged head gasket longevity.
compatibleVariants: GT W12, GT Speed W12, GT Supersports W12, GTC W12, GTC Speed W12, GTC Supersports W12
commonFailures: Head gasket degradation at 80k+ miles from repeated thermal expansion cycles
Valley pan coolant leak causing sweet-smelling vapour from cabin vents
Rear timing chain tensioner wear requiring specialist removal tooling
entries.cta: Get quotes for BR6 Bentley Continental GT engine replacement

entries.title: 6.0L Petrol
entries.history: Gen III W12 integrated 48V mild-hybrid tech, demanding precise ECU calibration for seamless torque-fill and emissions compliance.
compatibleVariants: GT W12 (Gen III), GTC W12 (Gen III), GT Mulliner W12, GTC Mulliner W12
commonFailures: PCV valve coking in stop-start urban driving causing oil consumption
High-pressure fuel pump wear requiring recalibration post-replacement
Starter-generator belt tensioner failure affecting 48V hybrid system
entries.cta: Get quotes for CRB Bentley Continental GT engine replacement

entries.title: 6.0L Petrol
entries.history: Early-production W12 code for 2003-2007 models; shares architecture with BR6 but with pre-facelift component specifications.
commonFailures: Valley pan gasket leak more prevalent on pre-2008 cooling system design
Early turbo actuator wear before TSB W12-15-03 updates
Spark plug access complexity increasing labour time for routine service
entries.cta: Get quotes for BEB Bentley Continental GT engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol
entries.history: Gen II V8 introduced cylinder deactivation and direct injection, improving efficiency but introducing carbon buildup challenges on UK short journeys.
compatibleVariants: GT V8, GT V8 S, GTC V8, GTC V8 S
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear per TSB SWE-19-07 on pre-2015 units
Intake valve carbon buildup from direct injection requiring walnut blasting
Electronic turbo actuator failure causing boost irregularities and limp mode
entries.cta: Get quotes for AWD Bentley Continental GT engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol
entries.history: Gen III V8 refined cylinder deactivation with updated lifters and hybrid-ready architecture, supporting both standard and Speed performance calibrations.
compatibleVariants: GT V8 (Gen III), GTC V8 (Gen III), GT Speed V8, GTC Speed V8, GT Mulliner V8, GT Supersports Hybrid
commonFailures: Cylinder deactivation lifter wear per TSB V8-20-01 on early Gen III units
Carbon buildup on intake valves exacerbated by urban stop-start driving patterns
Hybrid system interface calibration required for seamless 48V starter-generator integration
entries.cta: Get quotes for DTA Bentley Continental GT engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol
entries.history: Transitional V8 code used across late Gen II and early Gen III; shares core architecture with AWD/DTA but with updated fuel system components.
compatibleVariants: GT V8 (late Gen II), GT V8 (early Gen III), GTC V8 (transition models)
commonFailures: Fuel injector wear (Bosch 0280158987) affecting spray pattern and carbon prevention
Turbo oil return line coking requiring updated routing per TSB V8-17-02
PCV breather system blockage in high-mileage urban-use examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for CTA Bentley Continental GT engine replacement

engineGuide.closing: Can't find your engine code? Enter your registration number above - we'll instantly identify the exact unit, its technical specifications, and live replacement prices from UK Bentley Continental GT engine specialists. Every rebuilt engine comes with a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

commonProblems.h2: Bentley Continental GT Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Bentley Continental GT's W12 and V8 engines deliver exceptional grand touring refinement, but UK owners commonly report timing chain tensioner wear, turbo actuator failure, and carbon buildup on direct-injection V8s. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address these original failure points - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why Bentley Continental GT V8 Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Bentley Continental GT V8, V8 S, GTC V8 (2012-2018, AWD engine code)
problems.rootCause: Pre-2015 tensioners (OEM 07119556893) suffer premature wear under UK stop-start driving; TSB SWE-19-07 mandates reinforced replacement units.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Tensioner Replacement - Early intervention before chain stretch
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove front cover, replace tensioners and guides with updated OEM parts
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught before rattling noise; prevents catastrophic chain jump

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Full Timing System Overhaul - Chain, guides, tensioners replaced
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine front disassembly, full timing component replacement with calibrated tools
repairOptions.longevity: Extends engine life significantly; recommended for high-mileage V8s

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Complete unit swap
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt AWD engine with upgraded tensioners, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most cost-effective for engines with additional wear; eliminates repeat failure risk

problems.recommendation: On a Bentley Continental GT V8 worth £25,000-£45,000, preventative tensioner replacement at 50k miles offers excellent value. If rattling is already present or mileage exceeds 80k, a rebuilt engine with upgraded components typically delivers better long-term reliability than partial repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt AWD V8 engine prices for your Bentley Continental GT

problems.h4: Turbo Actuator Failure - Why Bentley Continental GT W12 Engines Lose Boost
problems.affectedModels: Bentley Continental GT W12, Speed W12, Supersports W12 (2011-2018, BR6/CRB engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Electronic wastegate actuators (TSB W12-15-03) suffer motor wear and position sensor drift, causing limp mode and inconsistent boost delivery.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Actuator Replacement Only - Single unit swap if diagnosed early
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo cover, replace faulty actuator with updated Bosch unit
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for isolated failures; requires precise calibration post-fit

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Turbo Reconditioning - Actuator, wastegate, bearings refreshed
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, full recondition with ceramic-coated components, bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for high-mileage W12s; addresses multiple wear points simultaneously

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt W12 Engine - Complete unit with upgraded turbos
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt BR6/CRB engine with ceramic-coated turbos, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for engines with additional wear; eliminates repeat turbo failure risk

problems.recommendation: For Gen II W12 Continental GTs showing boost irregularities, actuator replacement is cost-effective if caught early. However, if the engine has exceeded 80k miles or shows oil consumption, a rebuilt unit with upgraded turbos delivers superior long-term value and warranty protection.
problems.cta: Get quotes for BR6 W12 engine replacement with upgraded turbos

problems.h4: Carbon Buildup on Intake Valves - Direct Injection V8 Maintenance Challenge
problems.affectedModels: Bentley Continental GT V8, V8 S, GTC V8 (2012-2025, AWD/DTA/CTA engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Direct injection prevents fuel from washing intake valves; UK short-trip driving accelerates carbon coking, causing misfires and power loss (TSB V8-17-02).

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Walnut Blasting Service - Non-invasive carbon removal
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove intake manifold, blast valves with walnut media, clean ports
repairOptions.longevity: Effective preventative maintenance; repeat every 40k miles for urban drivers

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Injector Upgrade + Decoking - Address root cause
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace injectors (Bosch 0280158987), walnut blast, revise PCV breather system
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for engines with recurring buildup; improves long-term reliability

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt V8 with Updated Components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt AWD/DTA engine with upgraded injectors and breather system
repairOptions.longevity: Most comprehensive solution; ideal for high-mileage V8s with multiple wear items

problems.recommendation: For Continental GT V8 owners doing primarily short UK journeys, walnut blasting every 40k miles is prudent preventative maintenance. If misfires persist post-service or mileage exceeds 70k, a rebuilt engine with updated injectors and breather system offers better value than repeated partial repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt V8 engine prices with injector upgrades for your Continental GT

problems.h4: Cylinder Deactivation Lifter Wear - Gen III V8 Specific Failure Pattern
problems.affectedModels: Bentley Continental GT V8 Gen III, Speed V8, Mulliner V8 (2018-2025, DTA engine code)
problems.rootCause: Early Gen III lifters (TSB V8-20-01) collapse under cylinder deactivation cycles, causing valve train noise and potential camshaft damage.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Lifter Replacement Only - Targeted repair if diagnosed early
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove valve covers, replace collapsed lifters with reinforced OEM units
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if camshafts undamaged; requires precise torque sequencing

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt DTA Engine with Updated Lifters
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt DTA engine with reinforced lifters, calibrated oil pump, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution; eliminates repeat failure risk and covers ancillary wear

problems.recommendation: Given the low mileage at which DTA lifter failure occurs and the risk of camshaft damage, we recommend a rebuilt engine with updated lifters for any Gen III V8 showing valve train noise. The warranty coverage and comprehensive component refresh typically outweigh partial repair costs.
problems.cta: Enquire about DTA V8 engine replacement with reinforced lifters

problems.h4: PCV Valve Coking - W12 Stop-Start Driving Failure Pattern
problems.affectedModels: Bentley Continental GT W12 Gen III, Mulliner W12 (2018-2025, CRB engine code)
problems.rootCause: Stop-start urban driving accelerates oil vapour coking in PCV breather system, causing oil consumption and potential crankcase pressure issues.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - PCV System Service - Clean/replace breather components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove PCV valve and breather hoses, clean or replace with revised OEM parts
repairOptions.longevity: Effective for early-stage coking; recommended preventative service for urban drivers

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt CRB Engine with Updated Breather System
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt CRB engine with revised PCV system, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Comprehensive solution for high-mileage W12s; addresses multiple wear points simultaneously

problems.recommendation: For CRB W12 Continental GTs used primarily for urban commuting, PCV system inspection at 30k miles is prudent. If oil consumption exceeds 1L/1,000 miles or crankcase pressure codes appear, a rebuilt engine with updated breather components delivers superior long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Get quotes for CRB W12 engine replacement with revised PCV system

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Bentley Continental GT
finalCta.paragraph: Timing chain wear, turbo actuator failure, carbon buildup, and lifter collapse represent the most common failure patterns across Continental GT engine families. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Bentley Continental GT specialists who supply rebuilt engines with upgraded components addressing these original weaknesses. Every unit includes a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty, bench testing, and nationwide delivery - helping you preserve your GT's grand touring character without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Bentley Continental GT Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Bentley Continental GT and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Bentley Continental GT Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Bentley Continental GT is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement option.

groups.title: Bentley Continental GT Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Bentley Continental GT 4.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.0L twin-turbo V8 debuted in Gen II Continental GTs (2012), offering improved efficiency over the W12 while retaining grand touring refinement. Direct injection and cylinder deactivation introduced unique maintenance considerations for UK driving patterns.
items.cta: Compare Bentley Continental GT 4.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Bentley Continental GT 6.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The iconic 6.0L twin-turbo W12 powered all Gen I Continental GTs and remains available in Gen III models. Its unique architecture delivers seamless power delivery but requires specialist tooling for timing system and head gasket service.
items.cta: Get quotes for Bentley Continental GT 6.0L petrol engines

items.title: Bentley Continental GT Petrol Engines
items.description: All Bentley Continental GT models use petrol powertrains exclusively, with twin-turbo W12 and V8 configurations spanning three generations and multiple performance tiers.
items.descriptor: All Bentley Continental GT models use petrol powertrains exclusively, with twin-turbo W12 and V8 configurations spanning three generations and multiple performance tiers.
items.familiesLabel: Common Bentley Continental GT Petrol Engine Families
families: BR6 - 6.0L W12 Gen I (2003-2011), head gasket and tensioner focus
AWD - 4.0L V8 Gen II (2012-2018), carbon buildup and tensioner TSBs
CRB - 6.0L W12 Gen III (2018-2025), PCV coking and hybrid interface
DTA - 4.0L V8 Gen III Speed (2019-2025), lifter wear and deactivation calibration
foundIn: Gen I Coupe/Convertible (2003-2011) - BR6 W12 only
Gen II Coupe/Convertible (2011-2018) - BR6 W12, AWD V8
Gen III Coupe/Convertible (2018-2025) - CRB W12, DTA V8, hybrid-ready variants
knownFor: Seamless grand touring refinement and torque delivery across all configurations
Timing chain tensioner wear on pre-2015 V8 units requiring TSB SWE-19-07 fix
Direct injection carbon buildup on V8 intake valves exacerbated by UK short trips
High UK replacement demand on W12 head gasket and V8 turbo actuator failures

engineYears.h2: Bentley Continental GT Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Continental GT's production timeline (2003-present), organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: Gen I: BR6 W12 Launch Era
years.description: The original Continental GT introduced the 6.0L twin-turbo W12 (BR6), defining Bentley's modern grand touring character with seamless power delivery.
keyChanges: Launch of Continental GT coupe with BR6 6.0L W12 twin-turbo petrol engine
Introduction of Continental GTC convertible variant (2006)
GT Speed model added with uprated 600hp W12 calibration (2007)
Supersports variant launched with 630hp W12 and weight reduction (2009)
mainEngines: BR6 - 6.0L petrol W12 twin-turbo (GT, Speed, Supersports, GTC variants)
knownFor: Exceptional refinement and torque delivery characteristic of W12 architecture
Head gasket degradation from thermal cycling on high-mileage examples
Valley pan coolant leaks requiring gasket upgrade (OEM 070115271D)
Rear timing chain tensioner wear requiring specialist removal tooling
years.cta: Get quotes for 2003-2011 Bentley Continental GT replacement engines
years.ticker: Bentley Continental GT W12 head gasket replacement • BR6 timing chain tensioner fix • Continental GT valley pan coolant leak repair
enquiries: Bentley Continental GT W12 head gasket replacement
Continental GT valley pan coolant leak repair

years.preview: Gen II: V8 Introduction & W12 Refinement
years.description: Gen II introduced the 4.0L twin-turbo V8 (AWD) alongside refined W12 units, adding cylinder deactivation and direct injection technology.
keyChanges: Introduction of 4.0L twin-turbo V8 (AWD engine code) for improved efficiency
W12 engines updated with revised turbo actuators per TSB W12-15-03
Continental GT V8 S model launched with 528hp output (2014)
Timing chain tensioner TSB SWE-19-07 issued for pre-2015 V8 units
mainEngines: BR6 - 6.0L petrol W12 twin-turbo (GT W12, Speed W12, Supersports W12)
AWD - 4.0L petrol V8 twin-turbo (GT V8, V8 S, GTC V8)
knownFor: V8 direct injection carbon buildup on intake valves requiring walnut blasting
Timing chain tensioner wear on pre-2015 V8 units per TSB SWE-19-07
Turbo actuator failure on W12 Gen II causing boost irregularities
Improved fuel economy with V8 cylinder deactivation technology
years.cta: Compare Bentley Continental GT 2011-2018 engine replacement prices
years.ticker: Bentley Continental GT V8 timing chain fix • AWD engine carbon buildup service • W12 turbo actuator replacement cost
enquiries: Bentley Continental GT V8 timing chain fix

years.preview: Gen III: Hybrid-Ready Architecture & Performance Refinement
years.description: Gen III Continental GTs feature updated W12 (CRB) and V8 (DTA) engines with 48V mild-hybrid compatibility and refined failure-point components.
keyChanges: Introduction of CRB W12 and DTA V8 engine codes with hybrid-ready architecture
Cylinder deactivation lifter upgrade per TSB V8-20-01 for Gen III V8s
PCV system revisions to address coking in stop-start urban driving
Supersports variant returns with 771hp V8 hybrid powertrain (2024)
mainEngines: CRB - 6.0L petrol W12 twin-turbo (GT W12, GTC W12, Mulliner W12)
DTA - 4.0L petrol V8 twin-turbo hybrid-ready (GT V8, Speed V8, Supersports Hybrid)
popularModels: Bentley Continental GT V8 Gen III
knownFor: Cylinder deactivation lifter wear on early Gen III V8 units (DTA code)
PCV valve coking in urban-driven CRB W12 engines
48V mild-hybrid system calibration requirements for seamless integration
Enhanced emissions compliance with updated fuel and breather systems
years.cta: Enquire about Bentley Continental GT 2018-2025 engine availability
years.ticker: DTA V8 lifter replacement cost • CRB W12 PCV system fix • Gen III hybrid engine compatibility check
enquiries: Gen III hybrid engine compatibility check

faq.h2: Bentley Continental GT Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Bentley Continental GT engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt engine prices range from £6,200-£8,000 for V8 units and £8,500-£11,000 for W12 engines, supply-only, with supply & fit adding £1,200-£2,800 depending on workshop location.
keyPoints: V8 rebuilt engines average £6,200-£8,000 supply-only
W12 rebuilt engines average £8,500-£11,000 supply-only
Supply & fit labour typically adds £1,200-£2,800 to engine price
Prices vary by engine code, variant, mileage and supplier network
All EngineMarket quotes include minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Compare Bentley Continental GT engine replacement prices UK

items.question: What engine codes fit my Bentley Continental GT?
items.answer: Continental GT engine codes include BR6/CRB/BEB for W12 variants and AWD/DTA/CTA for V8 models; verify via VIN decoder or registration lookup for exact compatibility.
keyPoints: W12 codes: BR6 (Gen I), CRB (Gen III), BEB (early Gen I)
V8 codes: AWD (Gen II), DTA (Gen III), CTA (transition models)
Engine code stamped on block; verify via DVLA registration check
Hybrid-ready Gen III units require ECU calibration compatibility check
EngineMarket's lookup service confirms exact code match instantly
items.cta: Check your Bentley Continental GT engine code compatibility

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Bentley Continental GT engine?
items.answer: For well-maintained Continental GTs valued above £25,000, engine replacement typically preserves more value than part-exchange, especially with warranty-backed rebuilt units.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines cost 30-50% less than main dealer longblock replacement
Warranty coverage (12-month minimum) reduces long-term ownership risk
High-spec variants (Speed, Supersports, Mulliner) retain strong residual values
Consider vehicle mileage, service history and overall condition before deciding
EngineMarket specialists provide no-obligation valuation guidance
items.cta: Get expert advice on Bentley Continental GT engine replacement value

items.question: How long does a Bentley Continental GT engine replacement take?
items.answer: Typical turnaround is 5-10 working days for engine supply plus 1-3 days fitting at a specialist workshop, depending on parts availability and workshop scheduling.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines typically dispatch within 2-3 working days after order confirmation
Specialist fitting workshops require 1-3 days for complete engine swap
ECU calibration and road testing add half-day post-fit validation
Expedited service available for urgent requirements at premium cost
EngineMarket coordinates delivery and fitting for seamless handover
items.cta: Book your Bentley Continental GT engine supply and fit appointment

items.question: What warranty comes with a Bentley Continental GT replacement engine?
items.answer: All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering internal components, with documentation suitable for insurance claims.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty on all rebuilt/reconditioned units
Coverage includes timing components, turbos, fuel system and internal parts
Labour warranty available when fitted through EngineMarket specialist network
Warranty documentation provided for insurance and resale purposes
Confirm exact terms with chosen supplier before purchase
items.cta: View Bentley Continental GT engine warranty terms and coverage

items.question: Can I use a used engine for my Bentley Continental GT?
items.answer: Used engines offer cost savings but require careful verification of service history, compression data and mileage; rebuilt units provide greater reliability assurance with warranty coverage.
keyPoints: Used engines typically cost £4,800-£8,500 depending on mileage and condition
Request compression reports, leak-down tests and full service documentation
Verify donor vehicle history to avoid engines with known failure patterns
Rebuilt units include upgraded failure-point components and warranty protection
EngineMarket specialists help evaluate used vs rebuilt value for your situation
items.cta: Compare used vs rebuilt Bentley Continental GT engine options

items.question: Do I need ECU recalibration after a Bentley Continental GT engine replacement?
items.answer: Gen III hybrid-ready engines (CRB/DTA) require ECU adaptation for seamless integration; Gen I/II units typically plug-and-play with original harness if engine codes match.
keyPoints: Gen I/II engines (BR6/AWD) usually plug-and-play with original ECU if codes match
Gen III hybrid-ready units (CRB/DTA) require specialist ECU calibration
EngineMarket suppliers provide pre-flashed ECUs for simplified installation
Post-fit diagnostics and road test recommended to validate integration
Specialist fitting workshops include calibration in supply & fit packages
items.cta: Enquire about Bentley Continental GT ECU compatibility services

items.question: What are the common problems with Bentley Continental GT engines?
items.answer: Most frequent issues include timing chain tensioner wear (V8), turbo actuator failure (W12), carbon buildup on V8 intake valves, and PCV coking on urban-driven W12 units.
keyPoints: V8 timing chain tensioner wear addressed by TSB SWE-19-07
W12 turbo actuator failure covered by TSB W12-15-03
Direct injection V8s prone to intake valve carbon buildup on short trips
PCV valve coking affects stop-start urban-driven W12 engines
Rebuilt engines include upgraded components addressing these failure patterns
items.cta: Learn about Bentley Continental GT engine failure prevention solutions

items.question: Can a Bentley Continental GT engine be rebuilt to OEM specification?
items.answer: Yes - EngineMarket's specialist rebuilders restore engines to OEM+ specification using genuine or upgraded components, with dyno validation and calibration certification.
keyPoints: Rebuilds use OEM-spec gaskets, seals, bearings and timing components
Upgraded failure-point parts (tensioners, lifters, actuators) included where applicable
Dyno testing validates power output, oil pressure and emissions compliance
Documentation provided confirming rebuild specifications and warranty terms
Specialist calibration ensures seamless integration with vehicle electronics
items.cta: Compare OEM-spec Bentley Continental GT engine rebuild options

items.question: How do I find a specialist to fit my Bentley Continental GT engine?
items.answer: EngineMarket's vetted UK specialist network includes workshops experienced in Continental GT engine fitting, with supply & fit packages coordinating delivery, installation and calibration.
keyPoints: All network specialists vetted for Bentley Continental GT experience
Supply & fit packages include engine delivery, fitting and post-install validation
Nationwide coverage with transparent pricing and warranty coordination
Workshops equipped with specialist tooling for W12/V8 engine removal
Single-point contact simplifies the entire replacement process
